title: Power outlet cameras
abstract: A security system can be configured to mount to a power outlet. The security system can include a detection system comprising a camera, motion detector, speaker, microphone, and a pair of electrical prongs arranged and configured to protrude into the power outlet to receive power for the detection system. The security system can also include a remote computing device communicatively coupled to the detection system.
claims:
Claims THE FOLLOWING IS CLAIMED: 1. A security system configured to mount to a power outlet, the security system comprising: a detection system comprising a camera, motion detector, speaker, microphone, and a pair of electrical prongs configured to receive power from the power outlet; and a remote computing device communicatively coupled to the detection system, the remote computing device remotely located with respect to the detection system, wherein the security system is arranged and configured to detect a location of a last movement and display the location of the last movement on the remote computing device, and wherein the security system is arranged and configured to send a first alert to the remote computing device in response to the motion detector detecting no motion during a first predetermined time of day, and wherein the security system is arranged and configured to refrain from sending a second alert to the remote computing device in response to the motion detector detecting no motion during a second predetermined time of day that is different from the first predetermined time of day. 2. The security system of Claim 1, wherein the security system is arranged and configured to send the first alert to the remote computing device in response to the motion detector detecting no motion for at least a predetermined amount of time. 3. The security system of Claim 1, wherein the security system is arranged and configured to refrain from sending a third alert to the remote computing device in response to the motion detector detecting no motion during a third predetermined time of day that is different from the first and second predetermined times of day. 4. The security system of Claim 1, wherein the detection system further comprises a button that when pressed initiates a communication session with the remote computing device. 5. The security system of Claim 4, wherein when the button is pressed the camera captures an image of an area adjacent the detection system, and wherein the security system sends an image file to the remote computing device whereby the image file represents the image captured by the camera. 6. The security system of Claim 4, wherein when the button is pressed the microphone records an audible sound from an area adjacent the detection system, and wherein the security system sends an audio file to the remote computing device whereby the audio file represents the audible sound captured by the microphone. 7. The security system of Claim 1, wherein the security system is arranged and configured to emit a prerecorded message from the speaker in response to the motion detector detecting no motion for at least a predetermined amount of time. 8. A method of detecting a nonmotion event with a security system mounted to a power outlet, the security system comprising a detection system including a camera, motion detector, speaker, microphone, and a pair of electrical prongs arranged and configured to protrude into the power outlet to receive power, and a remote computing device communicatively coupled to the detection system, the method comprising: detecting, via the motion detector, the nonmotion event, wherein the nonmotion event comprises no detectable movement within a field of view of the motion detector; initiating a communication session between the detection system and the remote computing device in response to detecting the nonmotion event; detecting a location of a last movement and displaying the location of the last movement on the remote computing device; sending a first alert to the remote computing device in response to the motion detector detecting no motion during a first predetermined time of day, and refraining from sending a second alert to the remote computing device in response to the motion detector detecting no motion during a second predetermined time of day that is different from the first predetermined time of day. 9. The method of Claim 8, further comprising: determining, via the security system, whether the nonmotion event has occurred for at least a predetermined amount of time; and initiating the communication session between the detection system and the remote computing device in response to determining that the nonmotion event has occurred for at least the predetermined amount of time. 10. The method of Claim 9, further comprising emitting, via the speaker, a prerecorded message in response to the motion detector detecting the nonmotion event for at least the predetermined amount of time. 11. The method of Claim 10, further comprising: detecting, via the microphone, an audible sound; and initiating the communication session between the detection system and the remote computing device in response to detecting the audible sound. 12. The method of Claim 11, wherein the audible sound comprises a response to the prerecorded message. 13. The method of Claim 8, further comprising: determining, via the security system, whether the nonmotion event has occurred during a first predetermined time of day; and initiating the communication session between the detection system and the remote computing device in response to determining that the nonmotion event has occurred during the first predetermined time of day. 14. The method of Claim 8, further comprising: determining, via the security system, whether the nonmotion event has occurred during a second predetermined time of day; and 

 not initiating a communication session between the detection system and the remote computing device in response to determining that the nonmotion event has occurred during a second predetermined time of day. 15. A method of detecting a nonmotion event with a security system mounted to a power outlet, the security system comprising a detection system including a camera, motion detector, speaker, microphone, a button, and a pair of electrical prongs arranged and configured to protrude into the power outlet to receive power, and a remote computing device communicatively coupled to the detection system, the method comprising: determining, via the security system, that the button has been pressed; initiating a communication session between the detection system and the remote computing device in response to determining that the button has been pressed; detecting a location of a last movement and displaying the location of the last movement on the remote computing device; sending a first alert to the remote computing device in response to the motion detector detecting no motion during a first predetermined time of day, and refraining from sending a second alert to the remote computing device in response to the motion detector detecting no motion during a second predetermined time of day that is different from the first predetermined time of day. 16. The method of Claim 15, further comprising: capturing, via the camera, an image of an area adjacent the detection system in response to determining that the button has been pressed; and transmitting an image file to the remote computing device whereby the image file represents the image captured by the camera. 17. The method of Claim 15, further comprising: recording, via the microphone, an audible sound from an area adjacent the detection system in response to determining that the button has been pressed; and 

 transmitting an audio file to the remote computing device whereby the audio file represents the audible sound captured by the microphone. 18. The method of Claim 15, further comprising: receiving, via the security system, an input from the remote computing device; and initiating the communication session between the detection system and the remote computing device in response to receiving the input from the remote computing device.
